title: Nehvizdy

infobox.value: (Czech pronunciation: [[ˈnɛɦvɪzdɪ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Czech "Help:IPA/Czech"); [German](/wiki/German_language "German language"): *Nehwizd*) is a market town in [Prague-East District](/wiki/Prague-East_District "Prague-East District") in the [Central Bohemian Region](/wiki/Central_Bohemian_Region "Central Bohemian Region") of the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"). It has about 4,400 inhabitants.

["Population of Municipalities – 1 January 2025"](https://csu.gov.cz/produkty/population-of-municipalities-t4l3n8d2iw). [Czech Statistical Office](/wiki/Czech_Statistical_Office "Czech Statistical Office"). 2025-05-16.

["Public Census 2021 – basic data"](https://vdb.czso.cz/vdbvo2/faces/en/index.jsf?page=vystup-objekt-parametry&z=T&f=TABULKA&sp=A&skupId=4690&katalog=33475&pvo=SLD21043-CO). *Public Database* (in Czech). [Czech Statistical Office](/wiki/Czech_Statistical_Office "Czech Statistical Office"). 2022.
Profous, Antonín (1951). [*Místní jména v Čechách III: M–Ř*](https://mjc.ujc.cas.cz/search.php) (in Czech). p. 193.

["Historický lexikon obcí České republiky 1869–2011"](https://csu.gov.cz/produkty/historicky-lexikon-obci-1869-az-2015) (in Czech). [Czech Statistical Office](/wiki/Czech_Statistical_Office "Czech Statistical Office"). 2015-12-21.

["Population Census 2021: Population by sex"](https://vdb.czso.cz/vdbvo2/faces/en/index.jsf?page=vystup-objekt-parametry&z=T&f=TABULKA&sp=A&skupId=4429&katalog=33515&pvo=SLD21001-OB-OK). *Public Database*. [Czech Statistical Office](/wiki/Czech_Statistical_Office "Czech Statistical Office"). 2021-03-27.

["Nový památník seskoku operace Anthropoid má tvar padáku"](https://www.nehvizdy.cz/mestys/udalosti-v-mestysu/aktuality/novy-pamatnik-seskoku-operace-anthropoid-ma-tvar-padaku-1532cs.html?ftresult=v%C3%A1clava) (in Czech). Městys Nehvizdy. 2021-12-30. Retrieved 2022-05-04.

**Nehvizdy** (Czech pronunciation: [[ˈnɛɦvɪzdɪ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Czech "Help:IPA/Czech"); [German](/wiki/German_language "German language"): *Nehwizd*) is a market town in [Prague-East District](/wiki/Prague-East_District "Prague-East District") in the [Central Bohemian Region](/wiki/Central_Bohemian_Region "Central Bohemian Region") of the [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"). It has about 4,400 inhabitants.

## Administrative division

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nehvizdy&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Administrative division")]

Nehvizdy consists of two municipal parts (in brackets population according to the 2021 census):[[2]](#cite_note-2)

* Nehvizdy (3,877)
* Nehvízdky (40)

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nehvizdy&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Etymology")]

The name is derived either from the word *nehvízdat* ('not to whistle'), meaning "the village of people who do not whistle" or from the personal name Nehvizd, meaning "the village of Nehvizds (Nehvizd family)".[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nehvizdy&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Geography")]

Nehvizdy is located about 8 kilometres (5 mi) east of [Prague](/wiki/Prague "Prague"). It lies in a flat agricultural landscape in the [Central Elbe Table](/wiki/Central_Elbe_Table "Central Elbe Table").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nehvizdy&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/81/Nehvizdy_pomnik.jpg/250px-Nehvizdy_pomnik.jpg)](/wiki/File:Nehvizdy_pomnik.jpg)

Gabčík and Kubiš Memorial

The first written mention of Nehvizdy (regarding local [parish](/wiki/Parish "Parish")) is from 1352. Until the [Hussite Wars](/wiki/Hussite_Wars "Hussite Wars"), the village was owned by various lower noblemen. After the wars, it became part of the [Přerov](/wiki/P%C5%99erov_nad_Labem "Přerov nad Labem") estate. In 1515, Nehvizdy was promoted to a [market town](/wiki/M%C4%9Bstys "Městys") by King [Vladislaus II](/wiki/Vladislaus_II_of_Hungary "Vladislaus II of Hungary"). The period of greatest development occurred in the first half of the 17th century, but it ended with the advent of the [Thirty Years' War](/wiki/Thirty_Years%27_War "Thirty Years' War"), during which the market town was looted several times. After the war, Nehvizdy was acquired by the [Liechtenstein family](/wiki/House_of_Liechtenstein "House of Liechtenstein").[[4]](#cite_note-history-4)

On 29 December 1941, [Czechoslovak](/wiki/Czechoslovakia "Czechoslovakia") paratroopers [Gabčík](/wiki/Jozef_Gab%C4%8D%C3%ADk "Jozef Gabčík") and [Kubiš](/wiki/Jan_Kubi%C5%A1 "Jan Kubiš") sent by the Czech government-in-exile in London to [assassinate Reinhard Heydrich](/wiki/Operation_Anthropoid "Operation Anthropoid") were inserted near Nehvizdy (they overflew their predesignated target area near [Plzeň](/wiki/Plze%C5%88 "Plzeň") by more than a hundred kilometres due to a navigational error). The soldiers hid themselves in a nearby abandoned quarry and with the help of several local citizens were able to relink with collaborators and later fulfill the mission.[[4]](#cite_note-history-4)

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nehvizdy&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Demographics")]

The market town has experienced significant growth of population in the 21st century.

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nehvizdy&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Transport")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/24/Nehvizdy%2C_main_street.jpg/250px-Nehvizdy%2C_main_street.jpg)](/wiki/File:Nehvizdy,_main_street.jpg)

Main street

Nehvizdy lies on a road connecting Prague with [Poděbrady](/wiki/Pod%C4%9Bbrady "Poděbrady") and [Hradec Králové](/wiki/Hradec_Kr%C3%A1lov%C3%A9 "Hradec Králové"). Nowadays the [D11 motorway](/wiki/D11_motorway_(Czech_Republic) "D11 motorway (Czech Republic)") runs just south of the market town parallel to the old road.

## Sights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nehvizdy&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Sights")]

The main landmark of Nehvizdy is the Church of Saint Wenceslaus. It was built in the Gothic style in the late 13th century and was first mentioned in 1361. The adjacent bell tower dates from the 16th century.[[4]](#cite_note-history-4)

A small monument in the centre of Nehvizdy commemorates the Operation Anthropoid event. It is formed in the shape of a parachute and it was created in 2021.[[7]](#cite_note-7)
